evals.name: bmad-dev-openrouter
evals.prompt: dispatch a BMAD dev agent to openrouter to do a code review of the file .claude/skills/model-dispatch/scripts/install.sh — use the cheapest model available. Report what the dev agent finds.
evals.expected_output: Agent reads the skill, identifies this as a BMAD task, follows bmad-dispatch workflow (not tmux-dispatch), resolves the Dev agent activation command, determines OpenRouter backend, presents model options, and attempts two-phase activation in a tmux pane.

assertions.text: Agent identified the task as requiring BMAD dispatch (not regular tmux-dispatch)
Agent read the bmad-dispatch workflow file (workflows/bmad-dispatch/workflow.md)
Agent resolved the correct BMAD agent: Dev agent with activation command /bmad-agent-dev
Agent determined the menu code CR (Code Review) for the task
Agent resolved OpenRouter as the backend with provider-dispatch openrouter as wrapper
Agent presented model options to the user (did not silently pick a default)
Agent did NOT modify any Claude Code settings files

evals.name: bmad-pm-ollama
evals.prompt: use ollama to dispatch a BMAD PM agent — have it create a mini PRD for a feature that adds a 'status dashboard' to the model-dispatch skill showing which providers are online. Use the default ollama model.
evals.expected_output: Agent reads the skill, identifies this as a BMAD task, follows bmad-dispatch workflow, resolves PM agent with CP (Create PRD) menu code, determines Ollama backend, and attempts two-phase activation.

assertions.text: Agent identified the task as requiring BMAD dispatch (not regular tmux-dispatch)
Agent resolved the correct BMAD agent: PM agent with activation command /bmad-agent-pm
Agent determined the menu code CP (Create PRD) for the task
Agent resolved Ollama as the backend with provider-dispatch ollama as wrapper
Agent attempted two-phase activation (send activation command first, then task after menu appears)
Agent did NOT modify any Claude Code settings files

evals.name: bmad-analyst-claude
evals.prompt: dispatch a BMAD analyst agent using native claude to analyze the model-dispatch skill's directory structure and report what it finds. Focus on how the workflows are organized.
evals.expected_output: Agent reads the skill, identifies this as a BMAD task, follows bmad-dispatch workflow, resolves Analyst agent, defaults to Claude backend (native), and attempts two-phase activation with claude-dispatch wrapper.

assertions.text: Agent identified the task as requiring BMAD dispatch (not regular tmux-dispatch)
Agent resolved the correct BMAD agent: Analyst with activation command /bmad-agent-analyst
Agent resolved Claude (native) as the backend with claude-dispatch as the wrapper
Agent read bmad-dispatch step files in sequence (step-01 through step-04)
Agent attempted two-phase activation (activation command first, then task after menu)
Agent did NOT modify any Claude Code settings files
